title: shiftx semantics
body:
I am a bit confused with the shiftx semantics. I have this two modules and they fail LEC.

(This is more a topic for a dicussion/question channel like gitter, but I do not see any for yosys).

Gold model:
```
module gold_model(input [7:0] a, input [2:0] b, input [1:0] b2, output [3:0] c);
  assign c = a[$signed(b) +: 4];
endmodule
```

Shift based implemtation:
```
module gate_mode(input [7:0] a, input [2:0] b, input [1:0] b2, output [3:0] c);

  reg [3:0] tmp;
  assign c = tmp;
  reg [3:0]  bneg;

  always @(a or b or b2) begin
    bneg = -$signed(b);
    if ($signed(b)<0) begin
      tmp = (a<<bneg) | ((4'bx)&((1<<(-$signed(b)))-1)); // the | 4'bx.... part is not needed just for VCS match
      //UNCOMMENT to pass formal: tmp = a[$signed(b) +: 4];
    end else begin
      tmp = a>>($signed(b));
    end
  end

endmodule
```

After running lec with yosys, I get:
```   ______                   ___       ___       _ _            _ _
  (_____ \                 / __)     / __)     (_) |          | | |
   _____) )___ ___   ___ _| |__    _| |__ _____ _| | _____  __| | |
  |  ____/ ___) _ \ / _ (_   __)  (_   __|____ | | || ___ |/ _  |_|
  | |   | |  | |_| | |_| || |       | |  / ___ | | || ____( (_| |_
  |_|   |_|   \___/ \___/ |_|       |_|  \_____|_|\_)_____)\____|_|


  Time Signal Name             Dec       Hex           Bin
  ---- --------------- ----------- --------- -------------
     1 \gate_c                  --        --          0xxx
     1 \gold_c                  --        --          x000
     1 \in_a                     0         0      00000000
     1 \in_b                     5         5           101
     1 \in_b2                    0         0            00
     1 \trigger                  1         1             1
```

The yosys command is:
```
              design -copy-from gold -as gold gold
              design -copy-from gate -as gate gate

              miter -equiv -flatten -make_outputs -ignore_gold_x gold gate miter
              hierarchy -top miter
              write_verilog miter.v
              sat -tempinduct -prove trigger 0 -set-init-undef -enable_undef -set-def-inputs -ignore_unknown_cells -show-ports miter
```

I also have access to VCS, when I have this top level driver. I see that both generate the same results:
```
module top();

  reg [7:0] a;
  reg [2:0] b;
  reg [3:0] b2;
  wire [3:0] c;
  wire [3:0] cx;

  initial begin

    //a = 8'bx010x000;
    a = 8'b000x010x;

    b2 = 4;

    for(b2=0;b2<8;b2=b2+1) begin
      b = b2;
      #1 $display("a:%b b:%d c:%b cx:%b",a,$signed(b),c,cx);
    end
  end

  gate_model  i(a,b,c2,c);
  gold_model j(a,b,c2,cx);

endmodule
````

It generates this VCS result: (exact match)
```
a:000x010x b:          0 c:010x cx:010x
a:000x010x b:          1 c:x010 cx:x010
a:000x010x b:          2 c:0x01 cx:0x01
a:000x010x b:          3 c:00x0 cx:00x0
a:000x010x b:         -4 c:xxxx cx:xxxx
a:000x010x b:         -3 c:xxxx cx:xxxx
a:000x010x b:         -2 c:0xxx cx:0xxx
a:000x010x b:         -1 c:10xx cx:10xx
```

The 4'bx part is not needed. THis should be enough, but it was to make it match even with x's
```
    if ($signed(b)<0) begin
      tmp = (a<<bneg); /// The rest is just for VCS | ((4'bx)&((1<<(-$signed(b)))-1));
    end else begin
      tmp = a>>($signed(b));
    end
```

Is the SAT solver for the shiftx gate wrong? What is the semantic different? (Trying all options in vcs, they always match, so not sure why the SAT solver says to fail. Trying the failing inputs with vcs does not missmatch).
